name: Product cards

en.when_to_use: Use it when a small set of products, plans, or content items should become a scannable media-card grid showing image, title, price, tags, and rating, such as recommendation lists, catalog summaries, and comparison browsing. If the core task is rigorous side-by-side comparison against the same specifications, use comparison-table; if there are many records requiring filtering and sorting, use a data grid.
en.why_valuable: Ad hoc AI product cards often have inconsistent image ratios, misaligned prices and ratings, and tags that break the card; this template fixes the media ratio and information hierarchy, lays out responsively, and limits external images to data:image URIs or local graphic text to maintain zero network requests.

data_in.note: data.products is an array of card records. image accepts emoji/short graphic text or an embedded image URI beginning with data:image/; external http(s) images are not loaded. Numeric price is formatted for the current locale using currency, while string price is displayed verbatim. Only the first 4 tags are shown; rating is clamped to 0-5, and ratingCount is an optional review count.

schema.title?: string -- card-group title; displays "Featured products" when omitted
schema.currency?: string -- ISO 4217 currency code for numeric price; default USD
schema.products[].image: string -- emoji/short graphic text or a data:image/ embedded image URI; other URLs are not loaded
schema.products[].imageAlt?: string -- alternative text for an embedded image
schema.products[].title: string -- product name
schema.products[].price: number|string -- numbers are formatted with currency; strings are displayed verbatim
schema.products[].tags?: string[] -- tag chips; only the first 4 are shown
schema.products[].rating?: number -- 0-5 rating, shown as stars and one decimal place
schema.products[].caption?: string -- one short selling point or specification

spec.note: Uses an auto-fit grid with cards at least about 210px wide; automatically forms 1-4 columns from carrier width and fixes to one column below 520px. Height is determined by card count, with no external-media measurement.

when_to_use: 需要把少量商品、套餐或内容条目做成可扫读的媒体卡片网格，并同时展示图片、标题、价格、标签和评分时用它，适合推荐清单、目录摘要和对比浏览。若核心任务是按同一组规格严谨横比，应改用 comparison-table；若记录很多且要筛选排序，应改用数据网格。
why_valuable: AI 临时拼的商品卡常出现图片比例不一、价格和评分对不齐、标签撑破卡片等问题；本模板固定媒体比例和信息层级，自动响应式排版，并把外部图片限制为 data:image URI 或本地图形文本，保持零网络请求。

data_in.note: data.products 是卡片记录数组。image 接受 emoji/短图形文本，或 data:image/ 开头的内嵌图片 URI；不加载 http(s) 外部图片。数值 price 会用 currency 按当前 locale 格式化，字符串 price 原样显示。tags 最多显示前 4 个；rating 会夹到 0–5，ratingCount 是可选评价数。

schema.title?: string —— 卡片组标题；省略时显示 “Featured products”
schema.subtitle?: string —— 集合说明或推荐语
schema.currency?: string —— ISO 4217 货币代码，用于数值 price；默认 USD
schema.products[].image: string —— emoji/短图形文本，或 data:image/ 内嵌图片 URI；其他 URL 不会发起加载
schema.products[].imageAlt?: string —— 内嵌图片的替代文本
schema.products[].title: string —— 商品名称
schema.products[].price: number|string —— 数字按 currency 格式化；字符串原样展示
schema.products[].tags?: string[] —— 标签 chips；最多显示前 4 个
schema.products[].rating?: number —— 0–5 评分，显示为星标与一位小数
schema.products[].caption?: string —— 一句简短卖点或规格

spec.note: 使用 auto-fit 网格，单卡最小约 210px；随载体宽度自动形成 1–4 列，520px 以下固定单列。高度由卡片数决定，无外部媒体测量。

example._why: 四件风格统一的桌面用品用 emoji 作为零请求封面，覆盖多标签、整数/小数价格、评分和评价数，能形成完整而真实的商品目录。

products.title: K2 Low-Profile Keyboard
products.caption: Tactile switches in a compact aluminum frame.

products.title: Arc Task Light
products.caption: Flicker-free light with a warm evening mode.

products.title: Field Notes Set
products.caption: Three lay-flat notebooks with dot-grid pages.

uplink: 纯 display-only 组件，无 uplink；渲染结果只在当前 shadow root 中展示，不向 AI 或宿主回传状态。

carriers.artifact: supported:boot.artifact 已在 sandboxed iframe 中以 example.minimal 原样渲染验证；CSS 与 DOM 全部位于自有 shadow root
carriers.widget: supported:与 artifact 同一零依赖模板，仅 parent 改为 shadowRoot；可在载体 shadow 内再挂自有 shadow
carriers.codex: supported:与已验证的 artifact boot 相同，document.getElementById('viz') 为唯一载体挂载点

isolation.evidence: self-authored inline-template, owns its shadow, zero global footprint; rendered-verified
